chunks:
# ==============================================================================
# Chapter 16: Meta-Analysis and Comprehensive Results
# ==============================================================================
# Module-level intent for the sixteenth chapter of the IVD book. Final
# validation chapter in Part III: synthesize all results from Ch12–15 into a
# comprehensive summary table, perform cost-benefit analysis, identify ideal
# project characteristics, and address skeptic objections with data.
#
# Reference: book/book_system_intent.yaml (system)
# Parent:    book_structure.part_3_validation.chapters[5]
# Source:    Ch12 (Context Efficiency), Ch13 (Alignment Accuracy),
#            Ch14 (Knowledge Durability), Ch15 (Recipe Effectiveness),
#            Ch11 (Validation Framework), framework.md
# Version: 1.0
# Created: 2026-02-09
# Status: Pending
# ==============================================================================

parent_intent: "../../../book_system_intent.yaml"

scope:
  level: "module"
  type: "chapter"
  granularity: "coarse"

# ==============================================================================
# INTENT: What should this chapter accomplish?
# ==============================================================================

intent:
  summary: "Synthesize all validation results from Chapters 12–15 into a comprehensive meta-analysis—summary table of all IVD claims, combined effect analysis, cost-benefit calculation, project characteristic profiling, team-size scaling, compounding value over time, and data-driven responses to common objections."

  goal: |
    By the end of this chapter, the reader:
    1. Has a single summary table showing every IVD claim and its validation status (proven, partial, context-dependent)
    2. Understands the combined effect of context efficiency + alignment accuracy + durability + recipes working together
    3. Can calculate the ROI of adopting IVD for their team (investment vs long-term savings)
    4. Knows which project characteristics maximize IVD's value (complexity, team size, AI usage, lifecycle)
    5. Understands how IVD benefits scale with team size (solo → small team → large team)
    6. Sees the compounding effect: IVD's value increases over time as intent artifacts accumulate
    7. Has data-driven answers to the five most common objections against IVD
    
    This is the FINAL validation chapter. It bridges Part III (evidence) to Part IV (adoption).
    The reader leaves with complete confidence in IVD's value proposition, backed by data,
    and ready to make the case for adoption. The summary table is the chapter's signature
    deliverable—a single artifact the reader can reference, share, and use to justify IVD
    to stakeholders.

  success_metric: |
    - Reader can reference the summary table to answer "Does IVD actually work?" with data
    - Reader can calculate approximate ROI for their own team and project type
    - Reader knows when IVD is most valuable (and when overhead may not justify adoption)
    - Reader can defend IVD against the top 5 objections using specific data points from Ch12–15
    - Skeptic's Corner addresses real objections honestl

ata,
    and ready to make the case for adoption. The summary table is the chapter's signature
    deliverable—a single artifact the reader can reference, share, and use to justify IVD
    to stakeholders.

  success_metric: |
    - Reader can reference the summary table to answer "Does IVD actually work?" with data
    - Reader can calculate approximate ROI for their own team and project type
    - Reader knows when IVD is most valuable (and when overhead may not justify adoption)
    - Reader can defend IVD against the top 5 objections using specific data points from Ch12–15
    - Skeptic's Corner addresses real objections honestly—conceding where appropriate, strengthening the case
    - Chapter provides a clear bridge to Part IV: "Now that you know IVD works, here's how to adopt it"
    - All validation categories from Ch11 are accounted for in the summary table

  stakeholders:
    - "IVD book author (manuscript)"
    - "book/book_system_intent.yaml (part_3 purpose: prove every claim with data)"
    - "Ch11 validation framework (five categories), Ch12–15 results (individual validations)"

# ==============================================================================
# DELIVERABLES: Required sections (from book_system_intent Ch16)
# ==============================================================================

deliverables:
  sections:
    - id: "16.1"
      title: "Summary Table: All IVD Claims and Validation Status"
      required_content:
        - "Comprehensive table with columns: Claim, Category (context/alignment/durability/recipes/meta), Hypothesis, Result, Confidence, Chapter Reference"
        - "Every claim from Ch12 (context efficiency: 80-90% token reduction), Ch13 (alignment accuracy: 30-50% fewer iterations), Ch14 (knowledge durability: 90%+ retention, 40% faster onboarding), Ch15 (recipe effectiveness: 30-50% time savings)"
        - "Validation status for each claim: PROVEN, PARTIALLY PROVEN, CONTEXT-DEPENDENT — with clear criteria for each label"
        - "Meta-validation category (Ch11.6): does IVD improve its own governance? Self-referential evidence from the book project itself"
        - "Edge cases and limitations explicitly noted: where results were weaker, what conditions produced them"
        - "Statistical summary: confidence intervals, sample sizes, methodology references back to Ch11"
        - "The table must be the chapter's signature artifact: formatted for reference, sharing, and stakeholder presentation"

    - id: "16.2"
      title: "Combined Effect: Context + Alignment + Durability + Recipes"
      required_content:
        - "Individual effects are impressive; combined effect is transformational — explain why"
        - "The interaction model: how context efficiency enables alignment accuracy (less noise → better first output)"
        - "How alignment accuracy reinforces durability (well-aligned code matches intent → easier to understand after reset)"
        - "How recipes accelerate all three: pre-validated structure reduces context, alignment, and durability overhead simultaneously"
        - "Combined scenario walkthrough: a team using ALL four validated capabilities on a real project"

+ Durability + Recipes"
      required_content:
        - "Individual effects are impressive; combined effect is transformational — explain why"
        - "The interaction model: how context efficiency enables alignment accuracy (less noise → better first output)"
        - "How alignment accuracy reinforces durability (well-aligned code matches intent → easier to understand after reset)"
        - "How recipes accelerate all three: pre-validated structure reduces context, alignment, and durability overhead simultaneously"
        - "Combined scenario walkthrough: a team using ALL four validated capabilities on a real project"
        - "Quantify the combined effect: not additive but multiplicative (each capability amplifies the others)"

    - id: "16.3"
      title: "Investment Analysis: Upfront Cost vs Long-Term Savings"
      required_content:
        - "Honest accounting of IVD's upfront investment: learning curve, artifact creation time, process change friction"
        - "Breakdown by team role: developer time writing intents, lead time reviewing, team time learning the framework"
        - "Long-term savings: reduced iterations, fewer context resets, faster onboarding, recipe reuse across projects"
        - "Break-even analysis: at what project size/duration does IVD investment pay for itself?"
        - "Cost of NOT using IVD: accumulated technical debt from undocumented AI decisions, context loss at team transitions"
        - "Visual: investment curve vs savings curve over project lifecycle (description for diagram)"

    - id: "16.4"
      title: "Project Characteristics: When IVD Provides Maximum ROI"
      required_content:
        - "High-value characteristics: long-lived projects, AI-heavy workflows, multiple contributors, complex domains"
        - "Low-value characteristics: throwaway prototypes, single-person hackathons, trivial CRUD with no AI"
        - "The 'IVD fit score' concept: a simple rubric readers can apply to their own projects (complexity × team size × AI usage × expected lifecycle)"
        - "Real examples from Part II projects: which ones benefited most from IVD and why"
        - "The sweet spot: projects where IVD provides 5x+ return on the intent-writing investment"
        - "When to skip IVD: honest guidance on when the overhead isn't justified"

    - id: "16.5"
      title: "Team Size and IVD Benefits (solo vs small team vs large team)"
      required_content:
        - "Solo developer: IVD as AI communication protocol — your intent is your memory across sessions"
        - "Small team (2-5): IVD as shared understanding protocol — intents replace tribal knowledge and Slack explanations"
        - "Large team (6+): IVD as organizational knowledge architecture — intents are the system's living documentation"
        - "Scaling pattern: IVD benefits increase super-linearly with team size (communication overhead grows O(n²), IVD flattens it)"
        - "Data from Ch14 (durability) applied to team scenarios: what happens when team member 3 leaves and member 7 joins?"
        - "Practical recommendations: minimum viable IVD for each team size"

    - id: "16.6"
      title: "The Compounding Effect: IVD Val

- "Small team (2-5): IVD as shared understanding protocol — intents replace tribal knowledge and Slack explanations"
        - "Large team (6+): IVD as organizational knowledge architecture — intents are the system's living documentation"
        - "Scaling pattern: IVD benefits increase super-linearly with team size (communication overhead grows O(n²), IVD flattens it)"
        - "Data from Ch14 (durability) applied to team scenarios: what happens when team member 3 leaves and member 7 joins?"
        - "Practical recommendations: minimum viable IVD for each team size"

    - id: "16.6"
      title: "The Compounding Effect: IVD Value Over Time"
      required_content:
        - "IVD artifacts accumulate value: each new intent artifact builds on and references existing ones"
        - "The 'intent graph' grows: system → module → function; parent-child references create a navigable knowledge network"
        - "Month 1 vs month 6 vs month 12: how IVD value compounds as the artifact library grows"
        - "Recipe reuse across projects: a recipe validated in Project A saves time in Projects B, C, D"
        - "Knowledge preservation across time: code decays (dependencies, APIs), but intent survives (the 'why' doesn't change)"
        - "The flywheel: more intents → better AI context → better output → more value → more intents"

    - id: "16.7"
      title: "Skeptic's Corner: Addressing Common Objections with Data"
      required_content:
        - "Objection 1: 'Writing intents is overhead that slows us down' — Data: Ch15 recipes show net time savings; break-even analysis from 16.3"
        - "Objection 2: 'AI is good enough without intent artifacts' — Data: Ch12 baseline experiments show 80-90% unnecessary context; Ch13 shows 30-50% more iterations without IVD"
        - "Objection 3: 'This is just documentation by another name' — Structural difference: intents are executable (Principle 2), hierarchical (four levels), and AI-consumable; documentation is passive, flat, and human-only"
        - "Objection 4: 'Intent artifacts become stale and outdated' — Data: Ch14 durability results; Principle 4 (continuous verification) keeps intents alive; staleness is a process failure, not a framework failure"
        - "Objection 5: 'This only works for the author's projects, not mine' — Data: validation across multiple project types (agent, workflow, infrastructure, integration, full-stack); IVD fit score from 16.4"
        - "Each objection addressed with: the objection stated fairly, the data response (chapter reference), honest concession where appropriate, and final assessment"
        - "Tone: respect the skeptic; these are reasonable concerns. Answer with evidence, not rhetoric."

  manuscript:
    file: "chapter.md"
    required_elements:
      - "Key Points or Summary block at end (constraint: chapter_takeaways)"
      - "Key Points reflect book_system_intent Ch16 key_points: summary table, cost-benefit, project characteristics, ROI calculation"
      - "Summary table as primary deliverable (section 16.1) — formatted for reference and sharing"
      - "Checklist: 'IVD Adoption Readiness' — can the reader's project/team benefit from IVD? (constr

honest concession where appropriate, and final assessment"
        - "Tone: respect the skeptic; these are reasonable concerns. Answer with evidence, not rhetoric."

  manuscript:
    file: "chapter.md"
    required_elements:
      - "Key Points or Summary block at end (constraint: chapter_takeaways)"
      - "Key Points reflect book_system_intent Ch16 key_points: summary table, cost-benefit, project characteristics, ROI calculation"
      - "Summary table as primary deliverable (section 16.1) — formatted for reference and sharing"
      - "Checklist: 'IVD Adoption Readiness' — can the reader's project/team benefit from IVD? (constraint: chapter_takeaways, book_system_intent checklists in Ch16-20)"
      - "1–2 quotable lines where natural (constraint: quotable_principles)"
      - "Clear bridge to Part IV (Chapter 17: Getting Started: The First Week)"
      - "All measurements marked as illustrative from author experience unless backed by controlled study (constraint: no_unsourced_statistics)"

# ==============================================================================
# KEY POINTS (from book_system_intent Ch16; must be reflected in chapter)
# ==============================================================================

key_points:
  - "All IVD claims validated: comprehensive summary table with status, confidence, and chapter references for every claim from Ch12–15"
  - "Combined effect analysis: context + alignment + durability + recipes produce multiplicative (not additive) value"
  - "Cost-benefit analysis: honest accounting of IVD investment vs long-term savings, with break-even analysis"
  - "Project characteristics: IVD provides maximum ROI for long-lived, AI-heavy, multi-contributor projects"
  - "Team size scaling: IVD benefits increase super-linearly with team size (communication overhead flattened)"
  - "Compounding effect: IVD value grows over time as intent artifacts accumulate and recipes are reused"
  - "Skeptic's Corner: five common objections addressed with data, not opinion — honest concessions included"
  - "ROI calculation for teams: readers can estimate their own return on IVD investment"

# ==============================================================================
# CONSTRAINTS (from book intent; chapter-specific)
# ==============================================================================

constraints:
  - name: "chapter_takeaways"
    requirement: "Chapter ends with 'Key Points' or 'Summary' block and an 'IVD Adoption Readiness' checklist"
    source: "book_system_intent.constraints.chapter_takeaways"
    validation: "chapter.md contains a ## Key Points or ## Summary section plus checklist"
    test: "grep '## Key Points\\|## Summary' chapter.md returns at least 1 match; grep -i 'checklist\\|adoption readiness\\|before you move on' chapter.md returns at least 1 match"

  - name: "quotable_principles"
    requirement: "1–2 quotable, memorable lines in chapter where natural"
    source: "book_system_intent.writing_style.quotable_lines"
    validation: "Manual: at least one line is tweetable/memorable"
    test: "At least one bold standalone sentence in chapter.md works as a tweet/pull-quote"

  - name: "wr

raints.chapter_takeaways"
    validation: "chapter.md contains a ## Key Points or ## Summary section plus checklist"
    test: "grep '## Key Points\\|## Summary' chapter.md returns at least 1 match; grep -i 'checklist\\|adoption readiness\\|before you move on' chapter.md returns at least 1 match"

  - name: "quotable_principles"
    requirement: "1–2 quotable, memorable lines in chapter where natural"
    source: "book_system_intent.writing_style.quotable_lines"
    validation: "Manual: at least one line is tweetable/memorable"
    test: "At least one bold standalone sentence in chapter.md works as a tweet/pull-quote"

  - name: "writing_style_part_3"
    requirement: "Prose leads; code and intent-artifact examples illustrate. 'Prose explains and generalizes' — evidence and reasoning drive the chapter, tables and data support."
    source: "book_system_intent.writing_style.code_and_prose.part_1_3_4"
    validation: "Manual: prose leads, data/tables illustrate; reader is evaluating evidence, not building"
    test: "chapter.md has prose paragraphs driving each section's argument; tables, figures, and data appear as supporting evidence within prose-led sections, not as standalone deliverables without narrative context"

  - name: "formatting_conventions"
    requirement: "Follow book_system_intent.writing_style.formatting_conventions: bold for key terms, italic for emphasis, code for paths/commands, paragraph structure."
    source: "book_system_intent.writing_style.formatting_conventions"
    validation: "Manual: formatting matches conventions"
    test: "Key terms bolded on first use; code font for file paths and commands; no paragraph exceeds 10 sentences without subheading"

  - name: "evidence_based"
    requirement: "Every claim in the summary table must reference specific experiments from Ch12–15 with measurable results. No unsubstantiated claims."
    source: "book_system_intent.part_3_validation.purpose"
    validation: "Manual: every row in the summary table has a chapter reference and specific metric"
    test: "Summary table in 16.1 has no rows without a 'Chapter Reference' column value; each row cites a specific experiment result (e.g., '80-90% token reduction, Ch12.5')"

  - name: "summary_table_completeness"
    requirement: "Section 16.1 summary table must cover ALL claims from Ch12 (context efficiency), Ch13 (alignment accuracy), Ch14 (knowledge durability), and Ch15 (recipe effectiveness), plus meta-validation from Ch11."
    source: "book_system_intent.part_3_validation.chapters (Ch11–15 key_points)"
    validation: "Manual: cross-reference summary table rows against Ch11–15 key_points; no claim omitted"
    test: "Summary table contains at least one row for each validation category: context_efficiency, alignment_accuracy, knowledge_durability, recipe_effectiveness, meta_validation; total rows >= 5 covering all major hypotheses from Ch12–15"

  - name: "skeptics_addressed"
    requirement: "Section 16.7 must address at least 5 common objections with data-driven responses, honest concessions, and chapter references."
    source: "deliverables.sections[16.7]"
    validation: "Manual: 5+ objections, each with data response and c

)"
    validation: "Manual: cross-reference summary table rows against Ch11–15 key_points; no claim omitted"
    test: "Summary table contains at least one row for each validation category: context_efficiency, alignment_accuracy, knowledge_durability, recipe_effectiveness, meta_validation; total rows >= 5 covering all major hypotheses from Ch12–15"

  - name: "skeptics_addressed"
    requirement: "Section 16.7 must address at least 5 common objections with data-driven responses, honest concessions, and chapter references."
    source: "deliverables.sections[16.7]"
    validation: "Manual: 5+ objections, each with data response and concession where appropriate"
    test: "Section 16.7 contains at least 5 distinct objection-response pairs; each response cites at least one chapter/experiment result; at least 2 responses include an honest concession"

  - name: "checklist_present"
    requirement: "Chapter includes a checklist (e.g., 'IVD Adoption Readiness') as specified by book_system_intent for Ch16–20"
    source: "book_system_intent.constraints.chapter_takeaways (checklists in Ch16–20)"
    validation: "chapter.md contains a checklist section with actionable items"
    test: "grep -i 'checklist\\|adoption readiness' chapter.md returns at least 1 match; checklist contains at least 5 actionable items with checkboxes or numbered steps"

  - name: "master_intent_alignment"
    requirement: "Chapter content must not contradict ivd_system_intent.yaml or book_system_intent.yaml"
    source: "book_system_intent.constraints.master_intent_alignment"
    validation: "Manual review: Ch16 aligns with framework principles and validation methodology; no contradictions"
    test: "No principle, validation claim, or framework concept in chapter.md contradicts ivd_system_intent.yaml or book_system_intent.yaml"

  - name: "no_timeline_references"
    requirement: "Zero timeline references in chapter content (days, weeks, months) unless in examples/code"
    source: "book_system_intent.constraints.no_timeline_references"
    validation: "grep -i 'days|weeks|months|timeline' chapter.md (excluding code/examples) returns no matches"
    test: "rg -i '\\b(days|weeks|months|timeline)\\b' chapter.md outside fenced code blocks returns 0 matches"

  - name: "no_unsourced_statistics"
    requirement: "All measurements and ROI calculations must be marked as illustrative (author experience) unless backed by controlled study"
    source: "book_system_intent.constraints.no_unsourced_statistics"
    validation: "Measurement sections include caveat about illustrative data"
    test: "Sections 16.1, 16.3, 16.5 contain explicit caveat text (e.g., 'illustrative', 'author experience', 'not a controlled study')"

# ==============================================================================
# RATIONALE
# ==============================================================================

rationale:
  decision: "Chapter 16 is the meta-analysis synthesis — the final validation chapter. It aggregates Ch12–15 results into a single reference table, performs cost-benefit analysis, profiles ideal adoption characteristics, and addresses skeptic objections with data. Part III sequence: Ch11 f

about illustrative data"
    test: "Sections 16.1, 16.3, 16.5 contain explicit caveat text (e.g., 'illustrative', 'author experience', 'not a controlled study')"

# ==============================================================================
# RATIONALE
# ==============================================================================

rationale:
  decision: "Chapter 16 is the meta-analysis synthesis — the final validation chapter. It aggregates Ch12–15 results into a single reference table, performs cost-benefit analysis, profiles ideal adoption characteristics, and addresses skeptic objections with data. Part III sequence: Ch11 framework, Ch12–15 individual validations, Ch16 synthesis."

  reasoning: |
    - Part III purpose: prove every IVD claim with data. Ch16 is the capstone — the reader should leave Part III with complete confidence backed by evidence.
    - Summary table is the signature deliverable because: (1) it's the single artifact a reader can reference or share with stakeholders, (2) it forces completeness — every claim must be accounted for, (3) it mirrors best practices from systematic reviews and meta-analyses in research.
    - Cost-benefit analysis is critical for adoption: developers may be convinced by data, but engineering leaders need ROI. Ch16.3 bridges individual results to organizational value.
    - Project characteristics (16.4) and team size (16.5) prevent over-generalization: IVD is not universally optimal; honest guidance on when to use it strengthens credibility.
    - Compounding effect (16.6) is the long-term value proposition that separates IVD from one-time process improvements.
    - Skeptic's Corner (16.7) is unique to this chapter: it preempts objections the reader's colleagues will raise. By addressing them with data (not rhetoric), the reader becomes an informed advocate. At least 5 objections ensures thoroughness; honest concessions build trust.
    - Bridge to Part IV: with evidence complete, the reader is ready to adopt. Ch17 ("Getting Started: The First Week") is the natural next step.

  evidence: "book/book_system_intent.yaml (part_3, Ch16 key_points, placeholder); Ch11 validation framework (five categories); Ch12–15 results (individual validations); research/validation_methodology.md"
  date: "2026-02-09"
  stakeholder: "IVD book author"

# ==============================================================================
# ALTERNATIVES
# ==============================================================================

alternatives:
  - name: "Merge meta-analysis into each individual results chapter (Ch12–15)"
    rejected_because: "Loses the synthesis view. Individual chapters prove individual claims; the meta-analysis shows how they work together and provides the summary artifact. Without a dedicated synthesis chapter, the reader must construct the combined picture themselves."
    when_it_works: "If the book were shorter (< 15 chapters) and each results chapter already included cross-references and combined analysis."

  - name: "Place meta-analysis in an appendix rather than a chapter"
    rejected_because: "Appendices are reference material; the meta-analysis is a key argument in the book's per

h individual results chapter (Ch12–15)"
    rejected_because: "Loses the synthesis view. Individual chapters prove individual claims; the meta-analysis shows how they work together and provides the summary artifact. Without a dedicated synthesis chapter, the reader must construct the combined picture themselves."
    when_it_works: "If the book were shorter (< 15 chapters) and each results chapter already included cross-references and combined analysis."

  - name: "Place meta-analysis in an appendix rather than a chapter"
    rejected_because: "Appendices are reference material; the meta-analysis is a key argument in the book's persuasion arc. Moving it to an appendix signals 'optional' when it's actually the climax of Part III. The Skeptic's Corner especially needs chapter-level prominence."
    when_it_works: "If the meta-analysis were purely tabular data without narrative analysis or argumentation."

# ==============================================================================
# RISKS
# ==============================================================================

risks:
  - condition: "Summary table omits a claim from Ch12–15, creating an incomplete picture"
    action: "Cross-reference every key_point from Ch12–15 against the summary table rows; use constraint summary_table_completeness as the gatekeeper"
    monitor: "Verification cross-check #1; editorial review against Ch11 validation categories"
    severity: "high"

  - condition: "Cost-benefit analysis (16.3) feels fabricated or unrealistically favorable"
    action: "Mark all ROI calculations as illustrative from author experience; show sensitivity analysis (optimistic/pessimistic); be explicit about assumptions"
    monitor: "Reviewer feedback on credibility; constraint no_unsourced_statistics"
    severity: "high"

  - condition: "Skeptic's Corner comes across as dismissive of legitimate concerns"
    action: "State each objection fairly and strongly before responding; include honest concessions ('This is a real limitation when...'); avoid straw-man formulations"
    monitor: "Beta reader feedback from skeptical engineers; constraint skeptics_addressed"
    severity: "high"

  - condition: "Chapter is repetitive — rehashing Ch12–15 results without adding synthesis value"
    action: "Focus on what's NEW in this chapter: the combined effect, the cost-benefit math, the project profiling, the objection responses. Reference Ch12–15 for detail; don't re-explain experiments"
    monitor: "Beta reader feedback: 'I already read this in Ch12–15'"
    severity: "medium"

    current_step: "step_3_create_chapter_intent"
    completed_steps:
      - "step_1_verify_prerequisites"
      - "step_2_create_chapt

rity: "medium"

# ==============================================================================
# VERIFICATION (cross-check with book intent, validation chapters, IVD framework)
# ==============================================================================

verification:
  cross_check:
    - check: "All key_points from book_system_intent Ch16 appear or are clearly implied in chapter"
      validates: "intent.success_metric"
    - check: "All deliverables.sections (16.1–16.7) are present with required_content"
      validates: "deliverables"
    - check: "Summary table covers ALL validation categories from Ch11 and ALL results from Ch12–15"
      validates: "constraints.summary_table_completeness"
    - check: "Manuscript has Key Points/Summary block and IVD Adoption Readiness checklist"
      validates: "constraints.chapter_takeaways, constraints.checklist_present"
    - check: "Cost-benefit analysis includes explicit caveats about illustrative data"
      validates: "constraints.no_unsourced_statistics"
    - check: "Skeptic's Corner addresses at least 5 objections with data-driven responses and honest concessions"
      validates: "constraints.skeptics_addressed"
    - check: "Writing style: prose leads, data/tables illustrate (Part III style)"
      validates: "constraints.writing_style_part_3"
    - check: "Bridge to Part IV (Chapter 17: Getting Started) present in Key Points or closing section"
      validates: "deliverables.manuscript.required_elements"
    - check: "No contradictions with ivd_system_intent.yaml, book_system_intent.yaml, or Ch11–15 results"
      validates: "constraints.master_intent_alignment"
    - check: "All ROI calculations and team-size projections marked as illustrative where not backed by controlled study"
      validates: "constraints.no_unsourced_statistics"

# ==============================================================================
# CHANGELOG
# ==============================================================================

changelog:
  - version: "1.0"
    date: "2026-02-09"
    change: |
      Initial chapter intent for Chapter 16 'Meta-Analysis and Comprehensive Results'.
      Derived from book_system_intent.yaml Ch16 (key_points, placeholder), Ch11–15
      (validation framework and individual results), and framework.md (IVD principles).
      Sections 16.1–16.7 with required_content, 11 constraints (all with test fields),
      rationale, 2 alternatives, 4 risks, 10 verification checks.
      Part III writing style applied (prose leads, data illustrates).
      Chapter-specific constraints: evidence_based, summary_table_completeness,
      skeptics_addressed, checklist_present.
      Risks identified: incomplete table, fabricated ROI, dismissive skeptic tone, repetition.
    reason: "IVD: one intent per chapter; Ch16 is the final validation synthesis. Intent-only; manuscript pending."
